name: Housing Foundation Of America
description_short: HUD-certified nonprofit offering free homebuyer counseling, credit education, down payment assistance navigation, and foreclosure prevention across Florida and North Carolina.
description_long:
Housing Foundation of America, Inc. is a HUD-approved 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that has operated for 10 years, providing housing-related financial counseling and education. The organization focuses on making homeownership accessible through comprehensive support services rather than direct lending or credit repair.

The foundation offers a broad suite of free services including pre-purchase and post-purchase homebuyer counseling, credit and budgeting education, foreclosure prevention assistance, housing workshops, and connections to down payment assistance programs. They operate in multiple counties across Florida (Miami-Dade, Broward, Palm Beach, St. Lucie, Martin, Brevard, Lee, Orange, and the City of Orlando) and North Carolina (Guilford, Mecklenburg, Forsyth, Wake). Their 8-hour Fannie Mae-certified homebuyer course and virtual H2H Homebuyer Clubs provide structured pathways to first-time home purchase.

What distinguishes HFoA is their integrated approach combining credit counseling with down payment assistance navigation and property listings. They specifically highlight connections to down payment assistance programs (documented example: $74,000 from Broward County and $10,000 from Bank of America to recent clients), property opportunities through the Minority Builders Coalition, and reverse mortgage counseling. Their HUD certification and Fannie Mae framework compliance indicate regulatory credibility.

The honest assessment: while HFoA appears legitimate and well-structured, their website states "This website is not activated," suggesting current operational limitations or maintenance issues. Geographic service limitations (primarily South Florida and select North Carolina counties) and lack of detailed contact information beyond a single phone number (954-923-5001) make accessibility unclear. Their effectiveness depends heavily on local partnerships and down payment assistance program availability, which varies by location.
pros: HUD-certified nonprofit with documented 10-year operating history and 501(c)(3) status
Fannie Mae-certified 8-hour homebuyer education framework meeting industry standards
Documented down payment assistance success: recent clients received $84,000 combined from Broward County and Bank of America
Multi-service approach combining credit counseling, budgeting, foreclosure prevention, and homebuyer education in one organization
Operates across 12+ counties in Florida and North Carolina with property listings and GeoMapping tools
Free counseling services covering money management, debt resolution, mortgage delinquency, and loss mitigation
Reverse mortgage counseling available, addressing needs of older homeowners
cons: Website explicitly states 'This website is not activated,' indicating operational or maintenance issues that raise concerns about current service availability
Service areas limited to specific Florida and North Carolina counties; not nationally available
Limited transparency on counselor qualifications, response times, or service delivery details
Reliant on third-party down payment assistance programs which are not guaranteed and vary by location and income
Minimal contact information (single phone number provided); no email, live chat, or appointment booking visible
best_for: First-time homebuyers in South Florida or select North Carolina counties seeking free pre-purchase counseling
Homeowners facing foreclosure or mortgage delinquency needing loss mitigation guidance
Consumers rebuilding credit who also want homeownership education before pursuing down payment assistance
Older adults exploring reverse mortgage options with HUD-certified counseling
diagnosis: Housing Foundation of America is best for first-time homebuyers in their service areas (primarily South Florida and select North Carolina counties) who need free, HUD-certified guidance on credit improvement, budgeting, and down payment assistance navigation. The critical caveat is the website's statement that it is 'not activated,' which suggests potential current operational limitations—contact via phone (954-923-5001) to verify current service availability before relying on them for time-sensitive homebuying or foreclosure prevention needs.
services: Credit counseling and credit establishment/rebuilding education
Foreclosure prevention and loss mitigation counseling
Down payment assistance program navigation and application support
Homebuyer Clubs (in-person now virtual H2H format)
Home inspection tools and homebuyer counseling resources
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
